item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This seems like an excuse for a self insert and an excuse for using a drawing in one. There's not really going on with the anomaly or it's origin, holes that other comments mention even in almost non-existent scientific explanation. An intense focus on Nebula's personal life (which also pretty much doesn't exist) is in stark contrast to the cold and calculating nature of the foundation. Honestly I've seen better (imo) skips get discarded and I'd like to see this rewritten at the least.

As a personal aside I've been reading SCP for 6 years and I finally made an account because this broke the "suspension of belief" so hard for me and I wanted to voice that. Sorry, I don't want to discourage the writer and I believe this can be improved so it fits. This is all just my opinion

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A good ending,I like it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It a "nice" concept.

+1

P.S. Found out about you when Site-19 Instagram mentioned to you. Follow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Most of my issues with this have been explained in other comments, I'm just going to mention that it's painfully obvious that the attempt to justify using a drawing was bolted on, and it doesn't remotely fit with the other anomalous effects thematically.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Being slowly transformed from a sentient nebula to a teenager? Now that´s a horror story. + 1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No Vote. I don't dislike Kara as a character but I feel like there is no reason for them being apart of the story. This isn't a bad idea overall but I don't think this idea is explored to its full potential. I wanna see more stories about Kara but I don't feel that this SCP is the place for it. Because it comes across like your trying to tell two stories at once. One about a little girl who creates stars and the other about an SCPs relation to a researcher. If you wanna tell a story about your author's character and have them relate to an SCP than do it I have no problem with it. However, the problem I see with this is that there is no reason to include kara.

I feel like kara needs a bit more of a personality. Just being nice is not enough for me. What makes kara, kara. What are there wants and needs? What are there strengths and what are there weaknesses. What makes them who they are. The nicest person in the universe still has flaws we are imperfect by nature and that is what makes us human.

Sorry if this comes across as harsh. That was not my intent I think you as an author have tones of potential. You have amazing art and you are an amazing writer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's my issue, You don't sell she's a child in dialogue, so you'll have to sell it in different ways and by changing the image you show off a younger age, even if mentally.
"Its implied that she has had experience with these things even though she is so young in terms of star years." this is really not mentioned. I didn't assume that first thing, as won't most readers, a line like,

Researcher - "Wow, these drawings are very good! Where did you learn to draw like this?"
[Pause + Confused Look from the kid]
"I don't know, I just draw a lot!" (Shrugs shoulders)

And then you could go down a line of them talking about when the kid used to draw, like how she'd draw with the stars maybe, or maybe different mediums she drew with. Just some stuff to chew on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Except for the fact that she is 800,000 years old. Its implied that she has had experience with these things even though she is so young in terms of star years.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
is the fact that this child has literally NOTHING going on in her life besides a somewhat nice researcher. If you think about it, if you were to be isolated from society for a few years,

Okay well that would change this for sure, but I didn't know this until you told me this. It should've been specified in the article, this feels like a massive detail left out. Even an offhand line about lack of contact or saying that she got a new researcher that actually talks to her or something like that would've made it okay.

My issue with the image is that this is supposed to be a "self-portrait" and this doesn't look like a ten-year old's drawing. Ten-year-olds have a very unsure drawing style, which typically unsteady lines and inconsistent pen pressure. Trust me I have three nieces and they all like to sculpt and doodle with me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm disappointed in myself as a critiquer for allowing these issues to float through. In retrospect, a lot of these issues could have been avoided or at least assuaged if I had provided a more strict eye in regards to tone and coherency.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright, over all, good crit that I mostly agree with.

The reason for the praise and the journals having nothing else to focus on, is the fact that this child has literally NOTHING going on in her life besides a somewhat nice researcher. If you think about it, if you were to be isolated from society for a few years, then the few people that you've been around have made no attempt to talk to you or do anything for you, then suddenly someone does, you would probably do the same. Even from study from adults being in solitary confinement, there are irreversible effects on the brain. I've worked in a school as a student teacher, and I've watched children cling to their teacher or even me when there is nothing going on at home, or their parents don't give them enough attention.

Actually, I shaped this SCP on a girl I helped teach last semester. She was 7 I think, anyway she fascinated with stars and her parents weren't there. All she would write about was the teacher in charge of the class room and how much she wished the teacher would adopt her. I wrote from how she would talk and how she would act. All the teacher did with her was talk to her, much like how I made Kass act, and this girl became attached to this teacher.

With the picture at the end, I stated in my author post that it was soon to change.

Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Crit
- Close 

SCP-5827 is unable to appear in photography.

I might suggest that you add, “and any digital medium.”

our

Use “Milky Way”, “our” is not a clinical term. Milky Way would be better.

This causes

Maybe not this causes, but maybe, “While performing said ability SCP-XXXX will produce…”

creative nature

I wouldn’t use creative here, maybe “juvenile nature”.

So overall of the aforementioned section: tone needs some work, the image would probably work better if it is done by hand, also this doesn’t look like a ten-year-olds work, I understand that this is a very old ten-year-old and some can be talented, but changing it to be more childish could allow it to be more believable for it to be a child.

https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Lao_child_drawing_of_a_woman.jpg

The above image is an 11-year-olds drawing of a woman. I think it would be important to research and look into children’s drawings if you plan on redoing these images.

These interviews are very jumpy— which is fine talking with children tends to be that way, though too much so.

(They pull out a laminated pain chart.) What number would you say is the pain you feel right now?

Specify pain charts and maybe specify a location prior to this.

Alright, that's not good.

To me and most readers, 3 is like a soft allergies headache, you can live with it, because of this I feel like a 5 would be more eye-catching.

(Its nose starts dripping glowing blood. It notices and starts wailing)

This bit feels excessive, I would personally have built this up over time, and then maybe a tissue is offered, the kid asks why and Kara explains that they have a nose bleed, kid touches the blood, looks at it and cries.

Overall: Tone needs work, dialogue needs clean up and the journals feel like they are praising Kara too much, this isn’t bad if there was a reason behind it, but there isn’t. If you are going to have one character become emotionally attached to another then it has to be that way for a reason, like for example take 3604. In the document 3604 is saved by someone and begins spamming their emails, they’re shown to be starry-eyed and naive. This character doesn’t show off these traits well. I think that mastering the childish voice of the character would be enough though a bit more might be needed, such as past mentions of never being given stuff or maybe even mentions of having no one be able to help them and the Foundation was the only one to offer reliable help for it.

Aside from that, may I suggest the following media pieces to understand children’s thought process and voices:

Fran Bow - An excellent game series following a ten-year-old who has a mental illness, dialogue in the game is a bit strange, though it is all fairly childish, save for a journal that appears later in the game. Warning for minor gore that will appear in-game— you can find play playthroughs on Youtube.

Children’s Toys - A short film done by Dead Sound on Youtube - Follows a child in a dystopian society, I am also going to recommend that you watch the “Making of” video to understand the Creator’s intent.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=h2ByYKNXTsI - Children’s Toys

I can try to find more later though this is all I have currently.

Finally, I am going to suggest that you remove the crosslink to the article as it seems like you are currently trying to build onto a preexisting author avatar. IMO if you are going to have a crosslink, it should be relevant to the article, and the article shouldn't hinge on it, see the crosslink in 4003.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely, I understand that. I was more talking about the picture.

Again, I agree, I just can't make a lot of these edits currently as much as I would like to.

Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't find the explanation to justify why they used the drawing as the actual image for the SCP, and I think you would have been better off not having an image at all. You can write a child character without being a child yourself, after all we were all children once so everyone has some experience they can draw from. There's also plenty of writing samples that could be found to use as reference. It's possible to write a character different from yourself authentically, it just takes the time to learn what the voice that would work best would be. Timmy is a great example of how this can be done well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I understand what you're saying, but I can't change the story right now, major edits rule. I agree with you on some of this.

The issue is, is that I am not a child. I do not know any children, and I didn't find a picture that was compliant I liked. I gave an explaination (in universe) why she couldnt be photographed.

Thank you for your comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry you didn't like it.

As for your questions.

Nebula is not a self insert, and neither is Kass, despite the name. I actually had a hard time writing her because she is very different from my own personality.

I thought I had covered the fact she was solid? I'll check back on that.

The containment procedures say that "Solid or liquid material removed from the cell is to be treated with precautions appropriate to medium-level radioactive waste." Therefore it can be assumed that yes, they are radioactive.

The journal will degrade, just not yet. They got access when she was knocked out due to major pain. SCP-5827 dosnt entirely get angry. More opting to be sad.

She told them. Shes also the "reason" we call nebulas (ya know. The things that make stars?) nebulas.

I explained the mass thing. 2nd or 3rd paragraph of the discription. Yes, the journal is permitted by the Head Reasearcher and Site psyche staff.

Grammar error was purposeful, shes practically 10.

I hope that cleared some things up for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreeing with both of these comments, hence my downvote. There's stuff here to work with, and I'd be curious to see an eventual rewrite, but as it stands now there's not enough to justify an upvote.

As for my own feedback, I think the art is fine but doesn't work in the context of the journal - if its bright and colorful that means the skip has access to a variety of art supplies, which I'm not sure how that would get approval given the whole radiation and weight limit thing. Since it's a journal, maybe replicate a more sketchy style? As in colored pencils or ink pens? And if the skip is supposed to be 10, shouldn't the art reflect that as well? How much art experience do they have? Says they're creative but mostly focuses on making stars, not drawing. Apologies for nitpicking a lot on the art, but most of what I would point out with the writing has already been said.

Again, would be curious to see this further developed and potentially change vote, but for now, -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think these would all be very fruitful avenues to explore to make this SCP good, it makes no sense for the Foundation to not have this type of information in favor of the journal entries. Not saying you can't have journal entries too but more thoroughly exploring Nebula's anomaly and history would present plenty of opportunities to add depth and dimension to her character.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm really struggling to find anything positive to say about this. I think it's absolutely dreadful. The central character, Nebula, does not read authentically as a child and even in the attempt I think it's a waste of potential to make this character who is directly and literally made of and making starstuff into such a non-entity. Lines like "I can’t focus on anything but my body breaking. The stars want me back. I’m not doing my job." could work in a vacuum but in the context here it's very jarring to go back and forth from stuff like that to the little girl.

Other than the voice she's written in, the other major problem with Nebula is that she has no art, doesn't learn anything, and doesn't change because of the events of a story. She's a static character. Which is fine, you can write a static character in a way that works, but it's not working here. I don't think it was intentional, but the fact that the picture at the start and the picture at the end are palette swaps which don't actually show a significant change is more reflective of the actual article than was probably intended.

+ show block
– hide block

Nebula seems to be serving more as a vehicle to talk about the Dr. Kara character than herself. The journal entries are weighed down by Nebula repeatedly talking about how nice she is and her feelings towards this other character. It doesn't help that none of their interactions are particularly memorable or interesting. The SCP seems to be more concerned with promoting the Dr. Kara character than developing Nebula into her own person.

There are hints of character in the parts of the diary where she's not talking about Dr. Kara but that's only the March 17th section, the rest have to share space with talking up this other character who isn't even particularly relevant to the story. You could replace Dr. Kara with a generic Foundation scientist and the article would instantly become better because it would have more room for Nebula to show that she is her own person rather than a vehicle for showing how great another person is.

The Dr. Kara character contributes nothing and is actually detrimental to the article. All of her personal interactions with Nebula that aren't asking interview questions boil down to "Oh you like that thing? I like it too!" and the fact that Nebula seems to take so much meaning from these interactions cheapens the character because the audience doesn't care for these interactions, they don't add anything to drive a story or introduce conflict or anything else that makes character-driven stories fun to read.

I really dislike the picture, especially how it's very obvious that the second image is a palette swap of the first one. That's just my personal taste though, I don't think there's anything subjectively wrong with the image but the justification for why a photo can't be taken seems like more of an excuse to include the drawing than anything else, especially because it's not a core part of the article like, say, for Cassie where the SCP is literally a living drawing so there's not much else you could use as a picture.

There's a coming-of-age story in here somewhere but it's buried underneath a lack of relatability to the central character and too much space being taken up by a secondary character who really has no reason to be in the article at all. If you were to rewrite this I would remove Dr. Kara from it entirely, she adds nothing and every portion she is in detracts from the article, and try to make Nebula sound more like an actual little girl and not an approximation of one. Then focus more on her own internal struggle with this new thing happening to her. I think you could even keep the second image in the article as it makes sense in the second context of being a self-portrait in her journal. There are pieces here that could work. But it's just not working on any level right now.


As a courtesy to our readers on mobile devices, please collapse long posts. ~Zyn

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First of all, I see multiple links back to what I assume is a self-insert. Second of all, how does a nebula exactly interact with items (writing/drawing)? Is it that she is condensed enough that it is solid? Are the items radioactive after they're taken away? How does the journal not degrade from the radiation? How do the researchers have access to said journal without getting radiation everywhere? Do they decontaminate it? If so, how? How do they do it without SCP-5827 becoming angered? Why does the anomaly even have access to a journal? Who knew that it was called "Nebula"? A POI? Why was this never described? If the 4th-century thing is supposed to explain this, why do they know that this is the specific anomaly?

Its allowed books, so is the journal considered a book? Is that why it's approved? What about writing utensils? Why is no more then 10kg of mass allowed?

There's also a grammar error, just for your knowledge. "Stars, they are always very pretty." is not how that should be written. It should be written, in my opinion, as "The stars I made are pretty, which is why I made a lot of them". Or something along those lines.

I may have missed the point of the article, and I admit so, but these just broke the immersion for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Again, its really helpful when people do that, also thank you so much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mmm perhaps. I'm also kinda using this opportunity to try to refine my writing for SCPs as well lol, so I could be extra picky when reading through. Good work though, overall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're not the first person to bring this up to me, just a bit of a noticing thing I guess?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh no problem! I’m glad I was able to help!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean, you're totally free to not change it; I'm just one person anyway. From the vote count, it seems most enjoyed it, so I guess it's just me, haha.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I phrased that kinda wrong. You got what I was saying, and I'm going to try to fix that since re-re-reading it does give me the vibes of, 22 minute cartoon show, oh god, gotta wrap it up because I spent too long else where, if that makes any sense to you.

Thank you for the complement on the art, it took way too long lol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The ending was great, imo. It was more about the pacing in between the Star Journal-2 to -3; it felt… a little weird for some reason, when it felt like she really was in trouble in -2 but then the conflict was resolved… really fast? Idk, I felt like it was missing something in between -2 and the ending, so I think it could just be me. Again, the ending was nice, really. It sparked a bit of that warm feeling within me.

And also: your art's really great!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I see what you're saying, though a good bit of my crit was saying it was good with the ending, and some others requested more. I think its more of a reader interpretation type thing?

As for the nitpicky stuff, I quite appreciate it. Thank you, so much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ some general stuff
- hide

This was cute, really. It had a little bit of that warm, fuzzy feeling by the end. Though, I was a little bummed out that this felt quite a bit more… underwhelming than I expected? Idk, to me it feels… like the conflict ended… too early and hence felt a little rushed?1 From Star Journal-2 to -3, it felt like, there was kind of a gap in between that I was left out in the dark? I mean, it's understandable that they would provide the relevant amenities to 5827 when she was like, superheating the room and seemingly hurting her, but it felt… a little weird when it ended a little too early? So, it felt like, there was a bit of a weird blip in pacing, as we sense a little bit of that sadness that 5827 is feeling but then she suddenly sounds a lot better without a bit more of something in between? Idk.

I think this bit was the one sorta, holding me back a little bit from upvoting. Novote.

+ some very nitpicky stuff
- hide

SCP-5827’s secondary property is to move and create stars outside of our solar system.

"is to" sounds a tad weird here in this sentence; maybe "…property is the ability to move…"? Idk if "ability" and "property" are the same thing, so, uh, it's up to you.

SCP-5827: Um… Can I talk to you about something, Dr. Kara? Sometimes, my head starts to hurt and I feel like… I need to move the stars.

Idk but, the transition from the previous line to this line felt… a little off? Maybe something like a "(A pause.)" or something to give a bit of that breathing space? Her going from enthusiastic about learning science and then complaining about her pain sounded a little weird imo. And the foreword did say that Dr. Kara entered a conversation after 5827 began to like, say she had pains in her body, so it kinda felt… weird that 5827 starts off with books and only mentions the issue later on? Idk.

Footnotes
1. I'm not faulting you for rushing or stuff; it just felt like that to me :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey! Thank you so much! You were a great help on this! Though, again, thank you so much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good job on this! You've improved it a lot since I looked at it! I like this ending way better! And I love your art! Awesome job!

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Hello! Hello!

It's ya boy Kara, back at it again with— Wait. This isn’t a tale. It's an SCP!

That's right, today's your lucky day! That's right, I entered Corpsecon a while back to try my hand at writing a thing. Lucky for me, I managed to do it. Oh yeah!

Ok, that's enough celebrating. It was a rough week for me, getting my conprocs and summer classes starting, but I managed to pull though. I want to give a HUGE thanks to my critters TrustyOlValet2, cybersqyd, Dr Moned, MalyceGraves, Veralta, not_a_seagull, DianaBerry, TheSandwichMan, redredred, Lt Flops and DrAkimoto. They were a tremendous help towards this SCP and I love all of them.

As for the font, its on Google Fonts as ‘Shadows Into The Light’ so that's cool. I drew both of the pictures, though the second one is soon to change.

Anyway! Thank you so much for reading this and reading my SCP, I hope you enjoyed it!
